0C94141CF2560DBFBD8E06F6201C3D88: One or more area arm attempts by a user failed. Remote area arms attempts involve no user interface like the web interface or a terminal to provide detailed feedback for the failure, hence this message is created.

0D162C3DD8EE2088104323E67D379778: One or more inputs were placed into temporary isolation because they were not in a secure state when the area was armed. This means that the inputs were not being monitored at the time of the area being armed, and may indicate that they require attention in order to ensure the security of the system. This message ensures that the isolated inputs are brought to the attention of a user.
0E961EC324B0F1B3AFF642EE1F26899A: Specifies whether to exclude Users from the report if the system does not currently know what Area they are in (i.e. inactive users). NOTE: Only applies when the report is not filtered to specific Areas.
0EB710214CE3F03FB96D3BC3E55CDBBA: The first exception type is calendar exceptions. Calendars are focused on dates rather than days of the week and provide alternative start and end times to the regular recurring times of time periods for the dates defined within the calendar. Calendar exceptions are used when the same time change for a day should be repeated on multiple days throughout the year. The calendar can be updated, which could affect multiple time periods in a consistent way. A common use case example for calendar exceptions are extended trading hour days, where trading hours change consistently on many days throughout the year. The calendar in that case could apply to multiple time periods, like working hours, automatic door times and automatic arm times, each reacting to the same calendar. A user can then update the calendar throughout the year and the system reacts correctly. Another example is public holidays, a calendar can include holidays that that always fall on the same day each year, such as Christmas and New Year’s Eve and have them repeat, as well as being manually updated each year with holidays that change like Easter. The calendar exception could prevent the time period from being valid on a particular day and as such the normal operations would not apply for such things as user permissions or door free access times.

This allows more of the building to be monitored while everyone is asleep, while not immediately activating sirens if someone enters that section.

164339C64BC2606BE95C68496F7B5CFA: This option is used for a number of scenarios. If an input is faulty and repeatedly activates, or if it is externally accessible and an intruder can easily activate it, then repeated siren activations could annoy neighbours, cause people to eventually ignore the siren or potentially use up the siren activation limit of the area. With this option enabled however, subsequent activations of an input or module health issue would still be reported to the monitoring station, but the sirens won’t activate. Then, if a second input activates, the sirens will be able to sound and attract attention to the potential break in.
1654FF6806E1DC50452C5974B295FB44: , causing the system to ignore health issues from that module. This prevents health issues from that module from being reported to monitoring stations and also prevents users from being prompted about the health issues from that module when they attempt to arm areas. Once the health issue is fixed, the module can be de-isolated to return it to normal behaviour.

1B15D4A381B8186A562B2A07B8D24263: This trigger condition will be true when the door is in the 'Held Open Warning' phase. This occurs when the door locks while still open, and remains that way for the Held (DOTL) Time, entering a warning phase for Held (DOTL) Warning Time. This condition allows local responses to close the door before it is officially held open too long and alarm events are reported, etc. If the door closes, the door is unlocked again, or if that warning time expires and the door becomes 'Held Open', this trigger condition ends.

1F05C358317E8DC56ECE70210C9A09AA: , the key will function as normal, requesting access to the door for the PIN that was entered. Otherwise, the key will use the PIN entered to try to control the area instead of requesting access to the door. How the area is controlled will depend on the current state of the area. If the area is armed (including when entry or exit delay is active), an alarm is currently active or the area is about to arm, the system will attempt to disarm the area. Otherwise, the system will attempt to arm the area. The user who entered the PIN must also have the correct permissions for these actions to succeed.

213B2D3A5C5A05419E9C9F576E00CAAD: To edit an input click the input from the list and make the necessary changes and click the save button. Once the input is selected and can be controlled by clicking the control button this allows you to isolate or de-isolate the input. If an input is isolated, the area that monitors the input will not process the inputs state changes until the input is de-isolated.

2369247AB60F7281BD26C583AAB42FDA: Time periods are items that change between active and inactive based on the current date and time. 236F2EEA2191AF93CD133649CFB2CEB4: - When the input is triggered with the area armed, alarm sirens are played, strobes are enabled and an intruder event is sent to the monitoring station. This tamper response would occur regardless of the area's arm state

255C8C4956334B5A16336D83548F9149: The auto-arm inactivity time allows the area to be auto-armed if no activity is detected on the inputs within the area for a given period of time. This is commonly used to auto-arm rarely used areas without needing user interaction, ensuring they are secured more often. To set an auto-arm inactivity time, select the duration of time from the drop-down list.
255ED45EBC9932DC938DFC47EC1ED47D: Determines which input alarm events are reported to a monitoring station when they occur. Note that this only affects the reporting of input events, any siren responses will still occur regardless of the option chosen.
27837CD9D0A54BD96665BAA3C9FE7C17: Area walk tests are intended for routine maintenance of the system, allowing for periodic testing of the detectors and buttons within an area to confirm they are functioning correctly. Most importantly, this includes important but infrequently used inputs like medical, smoke or emergency buttons, as well as duress buttons.
2845014CE7856C30AC4849F72E9EDDC9: - When the area is about to automatically arm, the SIFER reader will sound the Area Auto Arm Warning tone and the Small LED will flash orange until the area is automatically armed or manually disarmed.
284970FC0A6DE23ABD4F16A5A7BB1A34: If the option is enabled, the Unit alarm state will be cleared as soon as all inputs within the secure unit are sealed or when the unit is unlocked. This allows the unit alarm state to follow the state of its inputs, but has the consequence that if a unit only has a door sensor then someone could open a secure unit door, enter, then close it straight away, and the unit would no longer be in alarm.
288D9B9F63911D4AD761B27B6522DA76: Permission groups are useful as they can include a common set of permissions that can then be assigned to multiple users. Once assigned, the permissions for all of those users can be updated by changing the permission group directly, making permission maintenance easy. When creating permission groups, it is recommended to first identify what is common between a number of users. This will generally be role based such as IT staff, admin staff etc. However on some sites it may simply be different level of access such as basic access or management access. The permission groups would then contain all the permissions required for a worker to access the doors and control the areas and outputs for them to do their job.

29A55625964832ACF7C3AA2B49B6C060: This step is used to configure the system to be able to send notification messages to users when events in the system occur. Notifiers can be configured, for example, to send notifications whenever an alarm occurs in a particular area or access to a particular door is granted or denied. Notifications can also be sent when repeated failed login attempts are made on the web interface or LCD Terminal, or if programming changes are made to particular entities. Maintenance events, such as power and battery issues of modules or RF devices can also be communicated.

These inputs will start the entry delay, allowing the user to disarm the system from a keypad or reader without generating an area alarm. Without a primary input being triggered first, the handover inputs would be treated the same as intruder inputs causing the area to go into alarm.

31EAB5F47254EB7FA08D58E8845D2F4A: To activate the feature, any of the inputs specified need to transition from an active state to a secure state. This is to ensure that the input has settled incase, for example, the input is a door reed switch or tongue sense that could bounce briefly when the door closes.
31F211424A9A6691524EFAF082B315E6: - If selected the input will function as a standard security detector input such as a PIR.  This input will require the detection device to be wired with EOL resistors to allow the system to identify alarm, seal and tamper states. Once configured, this input needs to be programmed into an area to monitor its state changes or used as a trigger for automation.
32BB4F941207A8C0315451F65042B8C1: If enabled, the SIFER/OSDP reader will provide feedback for the area located on the same side of the door as the reader. Feedback is provided for the following area states:
32F51F01B3B167638F30461A886D6CD1: Note that if using the IR-Fast with Text format, then area and input names within the system should be kept short as there is a limit on the number of characters that can be transmitted. There is a limit of 48 characters which must include the area and input names, along with the alarm event name.
34CFD4CD1B1F31C501E1C4FF629D02A2: This page is used to allow testing of inputs within areas, verifying that they are operating correctly. When active, input alarms will not be processed, allowing lesser used inputs like user duress or emergency buttons to be tested without sirens and alarms being reported.
3583E78DB7C4384AD9A1A8180A5FB516: These are the inputs that will immediately generate an area alarm. The intruder inputs should not include inputs that are located on the primary entry/exit path of the building. These inputs will generally be located on doors and in sections of the building that are accessed once the area is disarmed.
36950C17464D30D3E85B9C7FD75240AF: IR-Fast: A proprietary, Inner Range reporting format that can report area opens/closes and zone/input isolations, tampers, alarms and restores to a central station. Unlike contact id, no input or user mapping is required because all alarm reports and events can be reported uniquely.
37CE2367CAB71BDB8476003DF9CA8419: option below, as this alerts users that the area will re-arm soon. This option provides the user with time to either leave the area or disarm the area a second time.
3887316493E16B031E2162124CEF83B8: Tests can only be started when the area is disarmed and there is no active alarms in that area. Once started, a test will run indefinitely until it either successfully completes or is cancelled. A test can be manually cancelled at any time to return input processing back to normal and will automatically cancel if the area is armed while a walk test is running.
38E9A65E77BA834BF5A880A4EBBA7B57: option found on this page. In this case, the door will unlock when the time period is valid and the area is disarmed. Otherwise, if the followed area is armed or the time period is invalid, the door will remain locked.
39C1A6A90A8D34A8FAFC618BDE138CDF: - The alarm device reports its Path Status by controlling the state of a hardware output to represent when paths are available. This device output should be connected to Inception as an input and selected in the Path Status Input field.
3C08807D01B4F4D086CF246CA3413656: A time period can be selected to automatically arm the area. This can be used to arm an area late at night in case a user forgets to arm it. The area will automatically arm when the time period goes valid (start of the time period).
3DB151A35D0939F369B00152EBAE3FFB: page on the Inception web interface. This allows users to easily see which unit triggered the alarm, and allows monitoring stations to know when the alarm event has actually been restored.
3DB362D8A0F3BE57401405398E1D1AEB: option. In this case, the door will unlock when the time period is valid and the followed area is disarmed. Otherwise, if the followed area is armed or the time period is invalid, the door will remain locked.
3E5DC74AE2EE8C2E0FA9DDFA4F36C3DC: When enabled, the connected battery will be monitored for Low Battery and Missing Battery in the overall system health monitor. This option also allows the configuration of battery testing options below. The Inception Controller will report these hardware events to a monitoring station if alarm reporting is enabled and correctly configured, should the battery run low or a battery test is failed.
3E7D9F2AEDC4F8DD7F55B642A8EECF1A: For alternate door programming to be used, a schedule must be configured. The schedule can consist of one or more trigger conditions that evaluate to True or False (e.g. when a Time Period is Active, or when an Area is Armed), which are processed in combination to calculate whether the schedule should be active or not. The schedule logic can process the conditions such that the alternate mode schedule is only active when ALL of the conditions are true, or active when ANY of the conditions are true. When the schedule's state is active, the alternate set of options below will be applied, overriding the default programming for the door.
3EEEEF3F8AD49D15554D650848E87B60: - If selected, the input will function as a standard input for buttons or switches such as a duress point. This input will not require EOL resistors and it is just a normal two state switch or button input. Once configured, this input needs to be programmed into an area to monitor its state changes or used as a trigger for automation.
408970D02BFA521053DCDD3D9336721E: The terminal profiles determine which menus a user can view on the an LCD Terminal. The user can only control the areas, outputs and doors that their permissions allow them to, even if the profile lets them see the control menus.
40B756C15D649A36FF65271BC2B4E12D: Allows inputs to be added or removed from the area. Once an input is added you can assign the input an
43503E07C03EA484BECD3F8AC2A92DEA: . These start the entry timer and put the area in entry mode, allowing the user to walk along the path without triggering an alarm. This is generally going to be a door reed or a PIR near an entry door.
43A7DF085E7A8BCF2AE8A3FE656CD03E: This page allows quick and easy testing of the strobe and sirens linked to each area to verify they are connected and configured correctly. The manual control options can aid in diagnosing complicated issues as it can confirm whether the siren and strobe configuration within each area is correct.
453025350C38DA45F96333577718DCB1: This feature can be useful in systems with many areas, for example, by filtering to only the nearby areas for a given Touchscreen terminal. When a user accesses the terminal to control an area, they will only see the most relevant areas for that terminal, rather than having to scroll through every area in the system.
45908FF8FF9524578EBCB702F235FD06: Determines the amount of time that can pass after an unconfirmed intruder alarm occurs before the system is reinstated. To be compliant with the BS8243 standard, a time between 30 minutes and 1 hour must be selected. However, shorter times are available to aid with testing.
46DA531D920CC4695FCBE965CF326289: Isolates the module. This causes the system to ignore any health issues that this module could report, which could possibly prevent area arming, cause sirens to sound and be reported to monitoring stations. Used primarily when issues have been identified with the module that will take some time to fix. If an issue is expected to last for an extended period of time, it may be better to edit the module on the
4721492971A668CDF54BC845B7B71BA3: Determines what action or actions to perform when the trigger condition changes from false to true. These actions are edge-based and allow for the control of many things (outputs, areas, doors, inputs, readers, etc).
47423627D00699F2C9E5CA04C8178387: - Any disarm attempts that failed to disarm the area will be notified. The message will include which user attempted the disarm and which area they were disarming. Failed disarms occur due to insufficient permissions.
4756CFECB7E312C62B665889ED4DFC98: One or more inputs can be selected that are used to cut an active exit delay short. These are often used to prevent long exit delays from running for the full duration when the user has already left the area.
4A5CFF5B691546DC24EA06DB18FDC2B0: For example,  'Detecting Input Activity' for an area stays true while any input is active, preventing further triggers if one input remains active. 'Detecting New Input Activity (pulsed)' will trigger briefly for each new input activation, allowing actions like timed lighting control based on user movement. Generally, pulse conditions are used to trigger a set of actions when the event occurs.
4AFECBE7A14A9F11D221DA1F1B0F57B1: This trigger condition will be true whenever a user arms an area and triggers the exit delay which defines the amount of time they have to exit the area. The trigger condition will remain true for the duration of the exit delay which is configured in that area. Note that exit delay is only triggered on LCD Terminal arming or using the web interface option
4BAE08773822EC3C1A3CA9F6BCF3D19D: This trigger condition will be true while the selected area is armed.
4BEFD48D8A36493A1063F0A475CE8B44: action occurs for the unit, latching the alarm state. Clearing the alarm could be by the units tenant presenting their credential at the Block entry reader to unlock it, or by a User controlling the unit via the
4C789C883AFD72B6D70484658B5EC38C: If enabled, modules and alarm reporting paths will also be tested for any issues when an area arm attempt is made. If a user requested the arm, the attempt would fail if any issues are present and the user would be shown the reason for the failure and given the option to temporarily isolate them and try again. Alternatively, if the system requested the arm, the issues will be temporarily isolated (in the same way as inputs are temporarily isolated) and the arm will succeed. Any health issues isolated by the user or the system in this way will be automatically deisolated when the area disarms.
4DD3076B35CF7EDF1F95FC0D9AE9EF62: If enabled, the user can place the system into service mode, which will disable alarm reporting and siren responses.
4EB35A611BC1F0B39F1FC8B08F8EC8C5: Typically, arming via a remote would be done outside of the area, causing the area to arm straight away and prevent the need to wait for exit to complete. However in some circumstances, the area state indicators are within the area, requiring the user to be in the area being armed.
4FA0B9036575081039C85DF71E9DE4F9: If enabled, a user accessing this floor via a lift car will automatically disarm the
4FAD1D13E652C83205CB0657CE573F2E: This trigger condition will be true while there are one or more storage units in the chosen storage block are currently in tamper.
502F1607B1431FCA52232E41E590BA31: The Auto-Arm Inactivity Time Schedule field is an optional field that can be used to restrict the time that the Auto-Arm Inactivity Time field's logic is active, for example: you may want inactivity auto-arm to only occur outside of working hours. The inactivity time will be counted from the time the schedule becomes active, for example: if the schedule becomes active at 7pm and the Inactivity Time is set to 30 minutes, the area will only be able to arm at 7:30pm at the earliest. If no schedule is set, the auto-arm inactivity logic will always be active.
50C3D254CF3F5EDCB8D388B6B3606978: If configured, the Cabinet Tamper input on the module will be monitored by the system and will be included in the overall system health monitoring. This option determines the type of Cabinet Tamper switch that is connected to the hardware module.
5131270368D1338F0E4DF0DAFA8DEA8F: This trigger condition will be true whenever there is at least one active input in the selected area, staying true until there are no active inputs in the area. This occurs regardless of the area arm state. If triggering actions on each input activation is required, the new activation may be detected with the condition 'Detecting New Input Activity (Pulsed)' instead.
516AD46828287C0F096E9C2E179D7FD0: These inputs typically represent the intruder detection points that protect the perimeter of an area, such as door and window reed switches. Perimeter and Night arm modes would be used when people are inside the area, so internal motion detectors generally would not be included in this list.
51D3FE16037F4D855ED1F4AED80BF8B8: This trigger condition will be true while the area is in the 'Arm Warning' state, defined by the option
51FE54977E8FF69F71B2B810719B453C: When logged off, this terminal will beep with feedback messages to indicate that the area has an active alarm, that entry or exit delay is active or that the area is about to automatically arm. If a user logs on when one of these feedback messages are active, the terminal will automatically take them to the Control Area menu to allow quick control.
52692C21282D721EB8FC9DD6A1BF7363: If enabled, events from all areas will be monitored to see if a notification message should be sent. Otherwise, specific areas to monitor can be selected with the
528DCD495ABE741107760D2536678292: - Configures the output on the SLAM as a general purpose output that is not related to access control. This output behaviour allows the output to be used for intruder detection purposes such as a strobe or siren output, or for automation such as lighting control. Note: The SLAM outputs are limited in the amount of current they can switch, please refer to the SLAM installation manual for the contact ratings.
52D38C84933D4559B3496D05843D26F2: If enabled, the External Siren driver (S2EXT) on the module will be monitored for tampers and will be included in the overall system health monitor. To detect siren tampers, the siren devices need to be wired with a normally closed tamper switch.
530D615598A6351C11837FE6F42E2E13: If configured, the Cabinet Tamper on the module will be monitored by the system and will be included in the overall system health monitoring. The system will communicate this hardware event to a monitoring station if alarm reporting is enabled and correctly configured. In addition, all areas will go into alarm when a tamper is detected, regardless of whether the area is armed or not and will need to be disarmed to stop strobes and sirens sounding.
534B4BF1F5FF0408CFBE45A7060F78F2: When this is enabled, securing all active inputs within an area will clear the alarm state. Following this, an
53587ADBFEDBC2B00E8C76FB83F1443D: This trigger condition will be true while there are one or more storage units in the chosen storage block are currently in alarm.
53E44ABF85EDE5E6BC1AE2B26D646480: . While the exit delay or entry delay timer is active, Primary and Handover Inputs are ignored, allowing a user to walk along the path. If a Handover input is triggered before a Primary Input on the entry path (that is, the user bypassed the primary entry point), then the area entry time will be ignored and the area will go into alarm instantly.
560EEF3621F173BC076A5E63307BA969: - If selected, the input will represent the state of a repeater. The input will not go into the alarm state, however it will enter the tamper state if the repeater is tampered with, as well as monitoring for failed polls and low battery information.
5647C8484F04E158B07559802D62C6DA: This trigger condition will be true whenever entry delay is started by a user entering the selected area while it is armed, triggering an input that is assigned the
569506039AA258EF8AAC219E5E6E401C: - The SIFER will provide feedback on the state on the area located on the same side of the door as the reader. The reader will beep with different tones to indicate an alarm in the area, entry delay, exit delay and auto-arm warning. The small LED will turn red if the area is armed and will turn green when the area is disarmed.
579749A74554BF13811983D1A82A8036: option uses time periods and area states to automatically set the floor into free access, allowing a user to access the floor in a lift car without presenting a credential. This can be used to allow access to a floor during certain times of the day, but only if the area on that floor is disarmed. The floor will re-secure when a user arms the area on that floor or when the time period goes invalid.
5886C1EF1B3082C2ED7DB10B3D70C934: In addition, associating an area to the terminal allows that area to be armed and disarmed without needing to navigate to the Control Area menu. When logging on to the terminal, instead of pressing the
58D9043068FAC284F06BCB25EB7FE242: This trigger condition will be true if an Area Walk Test has been started through the web interface for the selected area, and will become false when the Area Walk Test has been cancelled or is successfully completed. Walk test mode is automatically cancelled if the area is armed while the test is running.
590779FD7DC3709D248C2117DFBF0CA5: Identify any automated responses that you want the system to perform and when you would want them to happen. They could be things like turning on lighting or air conditioning when the area is disarmed or when there is activity detected. They could also tie in to the security of the site, such as arming some areas when another area arms. Triggers can involve the state of multiple things in the system including time periods, so automation can also be set up to occur only during certain times. Depending on the automation required, multiple actions may need to be configured for the one task.
5A07095C66B72ED291E5C4EDE96FB29B: Allows users to be notified when input alarm events occur in the area. The areas that may be notified on are determined by the
5A802C69C3FA787FA61E4865FE90A232: option above is enabled. This option allows the perimeter intruder inputs of the area to be specified.
5A8575D19920D209EA3FA4AF5939DD94: This trigger condition will be true when the selected Hardware Module has detected external siren tampering, remaining true while the issue persists. This condition can apply to expansion modules with dedicated External Siren drivers.
5AC474EA01576A8EFE7732F4DD36D1EC: You need to assign a web page profile to a user to determine which pages the user can view and interact with. If the chosen web profile allows control of items such as areas, doors or outputs, the user will only be allowed to control the items that you assign in their user permissions. If the user only has permissions to control an area between working hours the same restriction applies to the control of the area from the web interface.
5B0F6992D04FF8F3088C4C37851EBEDF: This trigger condition will be true whenever an alarm is triggered for the selected storage unit, which occurs when there is an active input within a secured unit. This state will remain true until all inputs in that unit are no longer active or if the unit unlocks or is marked as vacant.
5B85734D8C226963A88DE25A8E2FCC4A: By default, doors will clear their override state when a free access time period changes or when an area arm state change occurs. This can be changed to only happen on one of these events occurring, or to never clear at all.
5BD9150577AF0CD36E8CC16BFBD151AE: Allows users to be notified when any health issues related to a module are overridden or isolated. Overriding of health issues causes the system to temporarily stop monitoring the module, often allowing areas to be armed.
5C1E96D6C4D319BF49E94B3A84C4D389: - Any user access attempts that are denied access through the door will be notified. The message will include which door the attempt was made at, who made the request and why the request failed (either due to the door being locked down, the area on the other side of the door being armed or no permissions)
5D23F07933480BDCE3B7056E0BB54811: If a user always presents their access credential when accessing doors, even if a colleague is holding the door open for them, then the user's location will always be accurate and they will never be stopped by anti-passback. Violations only occur when the user ends up in a different area than what the system knows. This could be by changing areas without presenting their credential, or presenting their credential at a door but changing their mind and not going through the door for example.
5D460218ECFF70C68AA28DE973357A0D: - Reports with the same alarm event type as a Duress input type, however alarm sirens are played and the area goes into alarm. Hold up events are processed regardless of the area's arm state. If tampered with, the same response will occur, except with a tamper event being sent to the monitoring station instead. This tamper response would occur regardless of the area's arm state
5D730C4105F8F61997B45C8BB04A037D: - Configures the output on the controller as a general purpose output that is not related to access control. This output behaviour allows the output to be used for intruder detection purposes such as a strobe or siren output, or for automation such as lighting control.
5D9BE4DC86D8EE5470E457EF65A394A3: This trigger condition will be true if the selected area has been configured with multi-mode arming and has been armed into the Full Area mode, turning false when no longer armed or armed in a different mode.
5FB4FEA3852DE4F94DA33FBC65AD272F: In addition to pulse counting, an area alarm will also be triggered if an activation is detected that remains active for longer than the pulse duration without restoring. This prevents pulse counting from being bypassed by an input activating but never restoring.
60562B1F8F6DEE944FEB0DF2C325C732: Note: Enabling this option creates a more secure system, where inputs on modules that have gone offline are treated as if they have been tampered with and are compromised. This could however cause undesirable behaviour (siren responses and alarms being sent to the monitoring station) if sections of the LAN are unreliable
60E9D41C2EB4D59A743464672DE2A6FC: It is possible to manually assign ID's, however in most circumstances this is unnecessary. Ensure that if you are assigning ID's manually that they are unique across all areas in the system, that is, no two areas share the same ID number. This also includes the system health area reporting ID located under
616117F5006AA07AD2603512C916AB46: The trigger condition can use the state of any area, door, input, output or time period and will create a trigger statement that is either true or false. For example, a trigger statement could be that the
61684B96C8228E6EFCBE2B4EE12E34DE: This step disables service mode that is enabled by default when first configuring the system. Service mode allows you to install and setup the Inception controller without generating sirens and alarm reporting. Once service mode has been disabled the system's sirens and alarm reporting will be enabled.
61EFDBA5B4609EDDA3E26BC87BA1EFA0: - Generates a report which shows a list of events where Users or the System have requested to arm or disarm Areas. Optionally filterable by Areas, Users, Arm/Disarm events, User/System events, and Successful/Failed events.
6327916A76B3DA6717F1E1FD4F940A93: page is enabled, any module health issues or alarms comms issues in the system will also be included in the pre-arm test. These issues would be automatically overidden and remain overidden until the area disarms.
659BF5D5EA002D4C54C18EE2FBA6470C: - Adds additional processing logic to support the EN50131 standard used in many locations throughout Europe. This adds many things to the system, most of which are internal to the system, while some additional options and features are shown. For example, changes to area alarm processing logic are included, additional Review Events and rules to how they are saved, rules applying to
65BCF2AD5A2E14C4F7D2675AC8AC1A36: Area notifications allow messages to be sent whenever arm or disarm attempts are made, or when alarm events occur. In addition, area arm and disarm validation can also be configured, with messages being sent if someone arms or disarms an area at the wrong time, or the area is not armed or disarmed when it should be. Specific input event types like alarm or duress events can also be chosen for monitoring, or events like low battery for RF devices can be sent to indicate potential maintenance is required. All areas in the system can be monitored, or specific areas can be manually chosen.
6636BF520789ED4C9FD08F84D19D1558: Allows users to be notified if an area is not disarmed at the correct time. This covers when a user or the system disarms an area at an invalid time, or when areas are not disarmed by a certain time. The areas that may be notified on are determined by the
665C74228052459DA058904964005838: This page allows you to quickly create every area needed for your site. The area creation is done separately from the area configuration to aid the
6780A9253A52CC182666E94EFB0AF6A4: Most security systems use End of Line resistors to determine if an input is in the alarm, tamper or sealed state. Inner Range products have historically used 2k2 and 6k8 or 2k2 and 2k2 ohm resistor pairs, however the Inception Controller and expansion modules can be configured to use other End of Line resistance values. This allows a security system from a different manufacturer to be easily replaced by an Inception Controller without changing the End of Line resistors found on every detection device.
6835F7E0793D8A4B2E6D22636C65A578: When this page is first opened, all the current locations for the Users are displayed and as the user location changes the page will update the location column. This page is informational only and does not provide any control options for the user. If the user does not have a location they have not entered a door programmed with inside or outside areas.
686BC74FD2A40E9DC4D5029366B8C2E0: button, this will open a window that contains all the available inputs. Once an input is selected you will be able to manually select an input type, it is recommended that you do not select primary, handover or instant alarm process groups.
6A3477006659C2C48E7E7A02B901AD17: This trigger condition will be true as long as the area has at least one active alarm of the chosen Filter to Alarm Type that has not yet restored. Note that this only relates to active alarms, for example, an intruder detector detecting motion in a disarmed area would not trigger this condition.
6A6F29AE8BEAE7BD20BD93CB98EAB008: This step is used to configure any automation logic that is beyond the basic intruder and access control system. Automated actions provide you with the ability to solve automation scenarios for the site. These would most often be simple automation cases such as turning on the lights when the area is disarmed, but have the flexibility to handle very complicated scenarios.
6ABA861C174F1124A1D4311681121087: This page is used to control when doors should automatically lock and unlock, when areas should automatically arm and when lift floors should be set to free access.
6B190428CCEA4FED588C31EF7136F1DF: This page is used to easily map inputs to areas.
6B3D0C4A73C2CF113916D7965915EF3C: If enabled, ensures that the LCD Terminal backlight is always enabled, even when logged off. This may be useful if the terminal is mounted in a dark area to have attention drawn to it or to be able to see the logged off display text.
6BB3B446BD51C8092BD089E702CD2716: input type, which will cause local sirens if that area is disarmed and sirens and a reported alarm event if that area is armed. This allows staff to control whether alarm events should be reported for the unit alarms, for example, leaving the area disarmed while staff are on site during the day, and arming at night for after-hours customer access.
6BCFACDA539B0B01471331C0CEEE8C6D: You can program the system with a single area or multiple areas. The benefit of a single area is the entire system is easy to arm and disarm. However, creating multiple areas allows you to divide the site and limit user access to certain areas, not every user will require access to the area like the Server Room.
6D0842360B34FF3621B0196100283A8B: Determines which outputs will activate to control strobes. Any input type that is capable of strobe control will turn the strobe output on, this output will remain on until the area is disarmed. To add a strobe output click the
6DEC6449FFCF82E1844DC02712B690B9: Alert feedback will only be allowed if the system is disarmed. If there is an area associated with the terminal (implying the terminals location), then alert feedback will be allowed if that area is disarmed. Otherwise, all areas in the system must be disarmed.
6E074D94325AA556BD44B0893E305085: option is inactive. This option allows a message to be sent if an area is armed outside of a normal schedule, for example, indicating that a shop closed early. The message will include which area was armed and who armed the area (either a user, an automated action or the system)
6E30229995BA49289A1532E2EC07BF0A: - Configures the input as a standard security detector input such as a PIR. Once this behaviour is selected you will not be able to link this input to a door. Instead, this is just a normal EOL input that can be programmed into an area to monitor its state changes.
6F0CE70631024F407BC3F30626505B17: This trigger condition will be true while the selected area is disarmed.
6F164CCFF750FB26340C77E07C026B42: inputs are usually wired to buttons or detectors that, if pressed, will cause the door to briefly unlock. Often placed on the side of the door where access control is not restricted, allowing the door to unlock to exit the door without presenting a credential or creating a door forced alarm.
6F866C913DB52A8423A6A52F53AD2CA5: Permission groups are generally created to suit particular roles on a site such as warehouse staff. The Warehouse Staff permission group would contain all the permissions required for a warehouse worker to access the doors and control the areas and outputs for them to do their job. If a new person was hired in the warehouse, you would simply assign them the Warehouse Staff permission group. The other benefit of using a permission group such as Warehouse Staff, is that if a new door was installed in the warehouse, this door would only need to be added to the permission group once rather than adding it individually to ten warehouse staff members.
70046CFBAF7F1FBAA91E3EE348F2F69E: If a reader tamper occurs, the system will communicate this hardware event to a monitoring station is alarm reporting if enabled and correctly configured. When reporting the event, the system will include the door's name that the reader is connected to, to help in identifying where the event occurred. In addition, all areas will go into alarm, regardless of whether the area is armed or not and will need to be disarmed to stop strobes and sirens sounding.
70BC65976B46F84C565E5A0B23DDBA3B: menu after logging into the terminal. System messages are informational, telling the user about events that happened in the past such as the reason for a recent failed area arm, a system reset or a health issue that has been restored, and just need to be acknowledged to remove them.
70CF74EA3615C5AEB6BFC29EE3F6F6B2: This determines the purpose of the reader's status LED. For SIFER readers, this is the small LED, for other OSDP readers, this is the only LED. This LED can be used to display a static colour or the door or area state.
71661C04D455B04C22E6DCB51D743841: Note: If a user has overridden the state of a floor to be secure or in free access from the web interface, the override will take priority. However, when the associated area changes state to armed or disarmed, the override (unless overridden to Lockout) is automatically cleared and the floor defaults to normal behaviour.
7189C00981889CC8A04DBD333752FDE9: This page is used to view and control the state of areas. To control an area you must have the appropriate user permissions and web page profile.
721CD04D2E09DF4C1F67EBAE1DEFF21F: If selected, a normally closed detection device or switch / button can be wired to the LCD Terminal. The LCD terminal input does not support end of line resistors and therefore can not identify the tamper state of a detector, it is therefore not recommended to use this input for security purposes. This input is commonly used for automation such as a button that performs an automated action.
73546DB42DD71A420616885E02B3F20B: - This is the six-digit serial number of the RF input, often located on the back or inside of the detector. If there is no number on the detector, you can identify the serial number by opening up the detector, waiting for its transmit LED to stop flashing and then pressing and releasing the tamper detection switch. You can then navigate to the
73E49EEB6C67E22986F704CF45ECD995: - If selected the input will function as a standard alarm detector. Device alarm and tampering will be monitored. This can be used with most transmitter devices like motion, smoke and glassbreak detectors. In addition, RF remotes could also be used if they are mounted as push-button inputs for wireless button inputs or user duress.
749536C47003FAF44AE5306FC8DF7041: If the system is used for intruder detection, this three-part step maps the inputs to the areas for monitoring, configures the areas, then tests the strobes and sirens to ensure their configuration is correct.
74FBD7A9C37803009FD604CFD2979B6B: An access control door is any electronically controlled obstruction, such as a hinged door, auto door, roller door or boom gate, that restricts a user accessing an area. The door and door related hardware are connected to the Inception Controller or Standard LAN Access Modules (SLAM).
7547C83309424CDDA849EA1CF8AC8997: Pulse state conditions are for moment-in-time events and are instantly set to 'true' then 'false' when the event occurs. Normal state conditions in contrast will transition to be 'true' upon meeting the condition and will stay 'true' until the condition stops being met.
75FA8BDBF82E11CA7E54140ACDB3E88F: You will need to assign the user individual permissions or the permission groups you created in a previous step, this will determine what areas, doors and outputs a user can access or control on the system and when they are allowed to control them.
7639C683058F690FA6F02D8A7ADEFDFB: If enabled, the system will have the duress user functionality. This enables a user that is under duress to type in the duress PIN number instead of their own. If the duress user has permission (configured below) they will be granted access as usual, however the system will generate a silent duress alarm event that will be reported to the monitoring station. This event will not generate area sirens or LCD messages.
76DFD4B4E640CE1ADB23CB8E62192305: Temporary isolations can occur if an input was not secure when the area was armed, requiring that the input be isolated so the arm could complete. Permanent isolations are also notified, often due to an input being faulty to prevent needing to temporarily isolate the input each time the area is armed.
772161AADF68220963B8E972601D9416: These are secondary inputs that are triggered as the user moves through the entry/exit path to disarm the area via a keypad or reader. If a primary input is triggered first and starts the entry delay, the activations on these inputs will be ignored for the duration of the entry delay. If the area entry delay is not triggered, the handover inputs will immediately generate an area alarm. These inputs should include most of the inputs that cover the entry/exit path, so if an intruder bypasses the primary entry point the alarm will sound as soon as possible.
778486B7108CB82900909A5810951700: If configured, the system will communicate this hardware event to a monitoring station if alarm reporting is enabled and correctly configured. In addition, all areas will go into alarm when a tamper is detected, regardless of whether the area is armed or not and will need to be disarmed to stop strobes and sirens sounding.
77BF2090BA0736DF7E677DF200FCDD61: Determines which additional outputs will turn on when a siren is triggered, this does not require the siren device above to be programmed. The siren output is commonly used to control piezo screamers. Once a siren output is defined and its location is set (internal or external), it will operate as per a siren device of the same type. To add a siren output, click the
78ED39352307741622A8C079829CF2AD: - If selected, the input's alarm/active state is swapped with the tamper state. This option is intended for sites where the majority of inputs follow the standard EOL logic, however some sensors (fire sensors, etc.) behave differently and require that their alarm and tamper state are flipped.
791B1D10F4362636567F6EB8D5612CF8: Note, this option should only be used after careful consideration, as it is a trade off between convenience for security and may be undesirable. Although it can prevent faulty inputs that are stuck in alarm or inputs that are slow to secure from sounding sirens, it could mean that the exit door could be left unsecure. In addition, the input or inputs assigned the
7953438613159D406AB95411E1725EDC: Generic Switch/Button * If selected the input will function as a standard input for buttons or switches such as a duress point. This input will not require EOL resistors and it is just a normal two state switch or button input. Once configured, this input needs to be programmed into an area to monitor its state changes or used as a trigger for automation.
7A2783D3DC4D9F3BCA1D2D287B7F7316: If all your doors are configured with inside and outside areas this page can give you an accurate indication of their last known location. In the case of an emergency such as a fire alarm, this page can be used to provide emergency services the user's last known location to assist in finding the user.
7AC594E0AA851FB029A4C198D69CA6F1: One or more level 3 users have logged into the system without level 2 user authorization. This was allowed because the user logged in locally via an LCD terminal and all areas in the system were disarmed.
7AF82E265B469F18E1101E468F85E56F: This step is used to create the permission groups that will be assigned to one or more users to determine the doors, areas and outputs the users will be able to access or control.
7C88FF090B18FA2BE267AE869F9CB155: If enabled, area inputs will automatically cancel an active Arm Warning state if they detect activity. The
7C969ACD6255EDB87848780218864B78: The system overview page shows summary information about the Inception System. Any issues that could keep the system from running optimally are also listed here and will cause a warning or error to display in the notification bar at the top of the page. Any warnings should be rectified as soon as possible. These could be indicating issues like modules or peripherals are offline or have health issues like power problems, or that an alarm reporting path is offline. Other warnings like inputs that are permanently isolated or doors which can not lock due to a broken emergency breakglass are also shown here.
7EA01249E95E64969EF185B9C941FF57: This trigger condition will be true whenever an alarm is triggered in the selected area, and will stay true until the area is disarmed.
80F7FF7FD21904D026C7F5DDFF5A4902: The End of Line configuration such as 2k2 + 6k8 determines the resistance values for the seal and alarm states. The first number 2k2 is the resistance that should represent a sealed input. The sum of both numbers 2k2 + 6k8 = 9k is the resistance for an input in alarm.
82105B21A04122BAAD07583B07789924: - BS8243 is in general an extension of the EN50131 standard. With this option, in addition to the features and processing logic added to support the EN50131 standard, the extra features and logic required by the BS8243 standard are enabled. On top of the existing EN50131 enhancements, the BS8243 standard primarily adds Confirmation logic to area alarm processing to differentiate between unconfirmed and confirmed intruder and hold up alarm events. Anti-code functionality is also included, allowing the ARC / monitoring station / alarm company to provide a level 2 user with a code to temporarily let them perform installer level actions.
82FEFC39E45BB09FC949937FAF4191BC: By default, the backlight on the terminal will be off when logged off, but will turn on when interacted with or displaying a message about the associated area or door state. The backlight can also be turned on briefly by pressing a button that does not have a function when logged off, such as the ON, OFF, MENU or an Arrow key.
8304C30B39F5F529736680E480C77B75: determines what the permission allows them to control and in what way, such as accessing a door or disarming an area. The
83066F71296ECC68C746F4D08F6BA272: means the task will not automatically execute itself at regular intervals, but doesn't necessarily mean that it is disabled entirely, as it can still be executed manually by users and triggered by automated actions. For example, a scheduled task could be configured to send a user location report as an email attachment, that is triggered by automated action when an emergency alarm occurs.
8355EAD0BC73879297BFB31F2774B180: This page simplifies the process of adding inputs to areas. This is done by adding the inputs to one of four categories. Three of these categories are related to Intruder Detection and will automatically assign the correct Process Group. This ensures that the inputs are processed correctly and the area functions as expected.
851BFB4027EDBA9C53388E1A3B8C5E9C: Note: This option can only be used for standard EOL inputs, it cannot be used for two state inputs (switch/button) as they do not have a tamper state.
85B31696D76BABF88281AE85F8EA92F9: The Inception controller can also utilize the Inner Range SkyTunnel architecture. SkyTunnel is a hassle free connection method that requires an internet connection for the Inception Controller and the connecting device. SkyTunnel is commonly used when access to the Inception Controller is desired via the Internet, for example, a user accessing the Inception web interface from a mobile phone in a different location. Use of the SkyTunnel architecture for internet access requires a paid subscription or an active alarm monitoring account.
85CFDC6594CE2BBE3BFBA0987E8CF000: If enabled, the External Siren driver will operate in DC driven mode, rather than driving the siren to make specific tones. This option allows a piezo screamer to be plugged directly into the siren driver point, rather than wired into an actual relay.
85E2E28F1CAD22E7D2DB57CBC34FEE58: If enabled, the Internal Siren driver will operate in DC driven mode, rather than driving the siren to make specific tones. This option allows a piezo screamer to be plugged directly into the siren driver point, rather than wired into an actual relay.
8785C32FD8A03F73F401C5D5D89D1D9A: To protect the storage units, they can contain motion or door position detectors to monitor unauthorized access to the space. If unauthorized access is detected, the unit enters an alarm state which can sound sirens and be reported to a monitoring station.
